title: Good Angel Bad Angel
wikitext:
[]: Good Angel - Bad Angel             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 23, 2005 11:03 am Yeallo :o . DirtyHamster's got another question for ya. I was doing some research on Angels and i came across this: Sandalphon: One of the Archangels of the Holy Sephiroth. and Lilith: One of the Unholy Sephira (found in the Kabbalah). This got me thinking. Are the Angels in Evangelion Holy* or Evil? Here's an example: Both Israfel & Leliel did not show any aggression until attacked first and Leliel only tried to communicate with Shinji. But Zeruel went on an absolute rampage. Sooooo although all the Angels are under God, where some sent by Iblis? *The reason i picked Holy, rather than Good, is because I consider it to be a more accurate antonym to Evil. Not because of its religious connection.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 23, 2005 11:12 am Eva is IMHO one of thouse shows were no ones really good or evil, just human. SO no I don't think the angels are either.

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 23, 2005 11:18 am What about Tabris? He was in no way human. He was completely Selfless.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Mon May 23, 2005 11:36 am something i'm not sure you're getting- none of the angels are human- they're angels

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 23, 2005 11:45 am Wait. Thats what I've being tring to say. And because their Angels they are either Holy or Evil, not human.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 23, 2005 11:47 am They are fundmentaly the same creatue as us remember misatos words from EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 23, 2005 11:48 am Doesn't the original Japanese name for the angels mean something closer to a "divine messenger" rather than an angel as we might think of it? Is this another case of us being thrown off by our in built preconceptions of christian nomenclature?

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 23, 2005 11:49 am DatDude wrote: They are fundmentaly the same creatue as us remember misatos words from EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 23, 2005 12:13 pm I think she said that they were humans that had rejected our human form. The DNA thing I think was just ment to show again how simaler we are to the angels on the most basic levels. Trust me I do believe in evil if I though the angels Id say so. I think profits was the closer translation of the word, but we'd need REichu to be sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 23, 2005 1:14 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Doesn't the original Japanese name for the angels mean something closer to a "divine messenger" rather than an angel as we might think of it? Is this another case of us being thrown off by our in built preconceptions of christian nomenclature?

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Mon May 23, 2005 1:19 pm Mr. Tines wrote: humans that gave up human form.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 23, 2005 1:26 pm DirtyHamster wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: humans that gave up human form.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 23, 2005 2:22 pm Perhaps it is meant to allude to the fact that humans posess the fruit of wisdom, and angels the fruit of life. Perhaps the angels chosse their quasi deific and near immortal, yet seemingly lonely and presumably sterile existence, over the inherently mortal, reproductive and evolving nature of the Lilim. A Keats might put it, they chose the "cold star"'s existence over their "fair loves ripening breast".

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon May 23, 2005 4:42 pm None of the angels actually bothered attacking human targets until they were provoked into doing so. Even in the case of Zeruel he was fired on first, of course he kind of over reacted but wasn't the first to strike. The angels arent out to get everyone, they just want to go back to Adam, of course if anyone trys to stop them, then you can count on some damage!

[]:             Quote Post by no common sense » Tue May 24, 2005 3:49 am The Angels don't seem so be inherently good (holy) or evil by nature since they lack a conscience. From the quasi religious perspective that the show alludes to, you could say the angels can't discern good and evil. Humans by eating from the tree of wisdom gained this knowledge but lost access to the tree of life. The Angels still have access to the tree of life but have never tasted the fruit of the tree of wisdom. It was because of the fall that man became by nature predisposed to evil. Since the angels never fell they would be neither predisposed to good or evil. Mind you my reference to man's predisposition to evil is in line with western thinking with evil being parallel with sin which is defined as falling short of the glory of God. The writers probably were more in tune with eastern thought which sees nothing as inherently good or evil. So really either way the Angels are neither.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 24, 2005 11:37 am If you want to get technical, Armisael and Arael both attacked first. I guess with Matarael and Sahaquiel, people just happened to be in the way. Also, keep in mind that these entities just happen to have the names of angels. They are not supposed to be the actual angels from which the names were taken. Assuming all Angels started their lives in the same way as Sandalphon, one can see what Misato meant about them "rejecting human form". Although I fail to see how the fact that a humanoid "evolves" into an octahedron means that Homo sapiens could have ended up as floating octahedra themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by DirtyHamster » Tue May 24, 2005 1:19 pm no common sense wrote: The Angels don't seem so be inherently good (holy) or evil by nature since they lack a conscience.

[]:             Quote Post by Crazy_Beserker » Thu Jun 23, 2005 8:32 am Aren't the angels here to prevent humans from playing god and thats the main reason for them attacking the world...the fact that they are called angels would seem to be just a reference or connection with god...wasnt the main reason of the second impact because of man trying 2 harness the power of god... i gotta watch the episodes again to be certain cause its been a while since i watched them so please by all means correct me if im wrong but thats the idea i seem to remember :? If thats the case they would be viewed as good because they are doing gods will but on the other hand evil in the ways they are going about doing it

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jun 23, 2005 8:55 am Reichu wrote: If you want to get technical, Armisael and Arael both attacked first. I guess with Matarael and Sahaquiel, people just happened to be in the way.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jun 23, 2005 11:15 pm as can be said of aramisael (i suck at spelling) communicating with rei- she/he/it was only trying to meld with her as a side note and i think reichu is kinda right on this one- the names are only names. I personally think that they were chosen to make the show sound cool. wasn't tabris the angel of death or something?
